{{Infobox Japanese Kofun | Name = Ogawadai Kofun Group | image = | Location = Ogawadai, Yokoshibahikari Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|20|N|140|29|40|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | Shape = Keyhole-shaped tumuli, round tumuli, square tumuli | Size = 28 m, 30 m, 42 m | Era = 5th century – 7th century (early 8th century) | Artifacts = Haniwa, others | Historic Designation = Town-designated tangible cultural property (haniwa artifacts) [https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a07 "History and Traditions" Yokoshibahikari Town Homepage] | Burial Structures = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Ogawadai
Kofun Group|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=20|lon_deg=140|lon_min=29|lon_sec=40|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|41|20|N|140|29|40|E}}}} }} '''Ogawadai Kofun Group''' (小川台古墳群) is a cluster of ancient burial mounds (kofun) located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Ogawadai Kofun Group is situated on a tongue-shaped plateau along the eastern midstream of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, in the central part of the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十九里浜}} region. It consists of 5 keyhole-shaped tumuli, 12 round tumuli, and 1 square tumulus. Among these, Kofun No. 1 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 28 meters), Kofun No. 2 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 21 meters), Kofun No. 3 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 24 meters), Kofun No. 4 (a square tumulus with a side length of approximately 14 meters), and Kofun No. 5 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 30 meters) were excavated in 1974. The largest mound in the group, Kofun No. 6, with a visible length of 42 meters, has yet to be investigated. == Artifacts == Significant numbers of artifacts were uncovered during the excavations of Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 5. - '''Kofun No. 1:''' Three burial facilities yielded four iron swords, one iron spear, four iron arrowheads, one iron knife, ten perforated circular stone disks, 39 spindle-shaped beads, three iron arrowheads, one iron axe, and more than 151 spindle-shaped stone beads. - '''Kofun No. 5:''' Artifacts discovered in a wooden coffin at the top of the rear circular portion include five long swords, one knife, one iron arrowhead, Hyōgo chains accompanying stirrups, one agate magatama (curved jewel), and 42 glass beads. The mound and surrounding moat contained numerous cylindrical haniwa, as well as human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, chicken-shaped, and waterfowl-shaped animal haniwa, in addition to house-shaped figurines and other symbolic haniwa. Analysis of their placement suggests that symbolic haniwa were concentrated along the northern middle section, extending from the front part of the keyhole-shaped mound to the rear circular portion, and were likely confined to specific sections of the mound. ### Human-shaped Haniwa Fifteen human haniwa were identified from Kofun No. 5, including figures of armored warriors standing with both legs, male figures standing with both legs, seated female figures, and women carrying jars on their heads. Of these, 14 haniwa were classified by gender, with 9 males and 5 females, making them the oldest known human haniwa in Chiba Prefecture. While these haniwa are somewhat rough in craftsmanship, their details—particularly the horned helmets, armor, and hair styles—demonstrate considerable fidelity to actual objects. Notably, the standing warrior and male figures are unique in that their feet are directly attached to the mound without a pedestal, a feature rarely seen elsewhere. Similar examples include the wrestler haniwa from Harayama Kofun No. 1 in {{ill|Izumizaki Village|ja|泉崎村}}, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture, housed in the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Museum|ja|福島県立博物館}}. These rare artifacts were designated as town tangible cultural properties on April 1, 2018.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/419372 Cultural Heritage Database] == Construction Period == The burial goods from Kofun No. 1 suggest a 5th-century construction date, while the haniwa from Kofun No. 5 indicate a period from the late 5th century to the early 6th century. Kofun No. 4 yielded {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} suggesting a late 7th-century to early 8th-century date, possibly associated with secondary burials. While only five of the 18 mounds have been excavated, the group reflects construction over an extended period, spanning from the 5th to the 8th century. == References == * {{ill|Hajime Otsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, Saburo Kobayashi, and Masaya Kumano (Editors), ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place Names (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 * {{ill|Reiji Harashima|ja|原島礼二}}, Ryoichi Kanaizuka (Editors), ''Eastern Japan and the Yamato Court,'' Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 1994. ISBN 4-642-02186-8, p. 141, "Human-shaped Haniwa of Ogawadai Kofun No. 5" == Related Topics == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Ancient Tomb and Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-4.html Shibayama Ancient Tomb and Haniwa Museum Exhibit on Ogawadai Kofun No. 5] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient Japanese burial mounds in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:小川台古墳群]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]]

Analysis of their placement suggests that symbolic haniwa were concentrated along the northern middle section, extending from the front part of the keyhole-shaped mound to the rear circular portion, and were likely confined to specific sections of the mound. ### Human-shaped Haniwa Fifteen human haniwa were identified from Kofun No. 5, including figures of armored warriors standing with both legs, male figures standing with both legs, seated female figures, and women carrying jars on their heads. Of these, 14 haniwa were classified by gender, with 9 males and 5 females, making them the oldest known human haniwa in Chiba Prefecture. While these haniwa are somewhat rough in craftsmanship, their details—particularly the horned helmets, armor, and hair styles—demonstrate considerable fidelity to actual objects. Notably, the standing warrior and male figures are unique in that their feet are directly attached to the mound without a pedestal, a feature rarely seen elsewhere. Similar examples include the wrestler haniwa from Harayama Kofun No. 1 in {{ill|Izumizaki Village|ja|泉崎村}}, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture, housed in the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Museum|ja|福島県立博物館}}. These rare artifacts were designated as town tangible cultural properties on April 1, 2018.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/419372 Cultural Heritage Database] == Construction Period == The burial goods from Kofun No. 1 suggest a 5th-century construction date, while the haniwa from Kofun No. 5 indicate a period from the late 5th century to the early 6th century. Kofun No. 4 yielded {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} suggesting a late 7th-century to early 8th-century date, possibly associated with secondary burials. While only five of the 18 mounds have been excavated, the group reflects construction over an extended period, spanning from the 5th to the 8th century. == References == * {{ill|Hajime Otsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, Saburo Kobayashi, and Masaya Kumano (Editors), ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place Names (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 * {{ill|Reiji Harashima|ja|原島礼二}}, Ryoichi Kanaizuka (Editors), ''Eastern Japan and the Yamato Court,'' Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 1994. ISBN 4-642-02186-8, p. 141, "Human-shaped Haniwa of Ogawadai Kofun No. 5" == Related Topics == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Ancient Tomb and Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-4.html Shibayama Ancient Tomb and Haniwa Museum Exhibit on Ogawadai Kofun No. 5] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient Japanese burial mounds in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:小川台古墳群]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]]

Kofun Group|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=20|lon_deg=140|lon_min=29|lon_sec=40|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|41|20|N|140|29|40|E}}}} }} '''Ogawadai Kofun Group''' (小川台古墳群) is a cluster of ancient burial mounds (kofun) located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Ogawadai Kofun Group is situated on a tongue-shaped plateau along the eastern midstream of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, in the central part of the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十九里浜}} region. It consists of 5 keyhole-shaped tumuli, 12 round tumuli, and 1 square tumulus. Among these, Kofun No. 1 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 28 meters), Kofun No. 2 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 21 meters), Kofun No. 3 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 24 meters), Kofun No. 4 (a square tumulus with a side length of approximately 14 meters), and Kofun No. 5 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 30 meters) were excavated in 1974. The largest mound in the group, Kofun No. 6, with a visible length of 42 meters, has yet to be investigated. == Artifacts == Significant numbers of artifacts were uncovered during the excavations of Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 5. - '''Kofun No. 1:''' Three burial facilities yielded four iron swords, one iron spear, four iron arrowheads, one iron knife, ten perforated circular stone disks, 39 spindle-shaped beads, three iron arrowheads, one iron axe, and more than 151 spindle-shaped stone beads. - '''Kofun No. 5:''' Artifacts discovered in a wooden coffin at the top of the rear circular portion include five long swords, one knife, one iron arrowhead, Hyōgo chains accompanying stirrups, one agate magatama (curved jewel), and 42 glass beads. The mound and surrounding moat contained numerous cylindrical haniwa, as well as human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, chicken-shaped, and waterfowl-shaped animal haniwa, in addition to house-shaped figurines and other symbolic haniwa. Analysis of their placement suggests that symbolic haniwa were concentrated along the northern middle section, extending from the front part of the keyhole-shaped mound to the rear circular portion, and were likely confined to specific sections of the mound. ### Human-shaped Haniwa Fifteen human haniwa were identified from Kofun No. 5, including figures of armored warriors standing with both legs, male figures standing with both legs, seated female figures, and women carrying jars on their heads. Of these, 14 haniwa were classified by gender, with 9 males and 5 females, making them the oldest known human haniwa in Chiba Prefecture. While these haniwa are somewhat rough in craftsmanship, their details—particularly the horned helmets, armor, and hair styles—demonstrate considerable fidelity to actual objects. Notably, the standing warrior and male figures are unique in that their feet are directly attached to the mound without a pedestal, a feature rarely seen elsewhere. Similar examples include the wrestler haniwa from Harayama Kofun No. 1 in {{ill|Izumizaki Village|ja|泉崎村}}, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture, housed in the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Museum|ja|福島県立博物館}}. These rare artifacts were designated as town tangible cultural properties on April 1, 2018.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/419372 Cultural Heritage Database] == Construction Period == The burial goods from Kofun No. 1 suggest a 5th-century construction date, while the haniwa from Kofun No. 5 indicate a period from the late 5th century to the early 6th century. Kofun No. 4 yielded {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} suggesting a late 7th-century to early 8th-century date, possibly associated with secondary burials. While only five of the 18 mounds have been excavated, the group reflects construction over an extended period, spanning from the 5th to the 8th century. == References == * {{ill|Hajime Otsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, Saburo Kobayashi, and Masaya Kumano (Editors), ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place Names (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 * {{ill|Reiji Harashima|ja|原島礼二}}, Ryoichi Kanaizuka (Editors), ''Eastern Japan and the Yamato Court,'' Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 1994. ISBN 4-642-02186-8, p. 141, "Human-shaped Haniwa of Ogawadai Kofun No. 5" == Related Topics == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-4.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibit on Ogawadai Kofun No. 5] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient Japanese burial mounds in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:小川台古墳群]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]]

ISBN 4-642-02186-8, p. 141, "Human-shaped Haniwa of Ogawadai Kofun No. 5" == Related Topics == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-4.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibit on Ogawadai Kofun No. 5] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient Japanese burial mounds in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:小川台古墳群]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox Japanese Kofun | Name = Ogawadai Kofun Group | image = | Location = Ogawadai, Yokoshibahikari Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|20|N|140|29|40|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | Shape = Keyhole-shaped tumuli, round tumuli, square tumuli | Size = 28 m, 30 m, 42 m | Era = 5th century – 7th century (early 8th century) | Artifacts = Haniwa, others | Historic Designation = Town-designated tangible cultural property (haniwa artifacts) [https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a07 "History and Traditions" Yokoshibahikari Town Homepage] | Burial Structures = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Ogawadai
Kofun Group|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=20|lon_deg=140|lon_min=29|lon_sec=40|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|41|20|N|140|29|40|E}}}} }} '''Ogawadai Kofun Group''' (小川台古墳群) is a cluster of ancient burial mounds (kofun) located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Ogawadai Kofun Group is situated on a tongue-shaped plateau along the eastern midstream of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, in the central part of the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十九里浜}} region. It consists of 5 keyhole-shaped tumuli, 12 round tumuli, and 1 square tumulus. Among these, Kofun No. 1 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 28 meters), Kofun No. 2 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 21 meters), Kofun No. 3 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 24 meters), Kofun No. 4 (a square tumulus with a side length of approximately 14 meters), and Kofun No. 5 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 30 meters) were excavated in 1974. The largest mound in the group, Kofun No. 6, with a visible length of 42 meters, has yet to be investigated. == Artifacts == Significant numbers of artifacts were uncovered during the excavations of Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 5. - '''Kofun No. 1:''' Three burial facilities yielded four iron swords, one iron spear, four iron arrowheads, one iron knife, ten perforated circular stone disks, 39 spindle-shaped beads, three iron arrowheads, one iron axe, and more than 151 spindle-shaped stone beads. - '''Kofun No. 5:''' Artifacts discovered in a wooden coffin at the top of the rear circular portion include five long swords, one knife, one iron arrowhead, Hyōgo chains accompanying stirrups, one agate magatama (curved jewel), and 42 glass beads. The mound and surrounding moat contained numerous cylindrical haniwa, as well as human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, chicken-shaped, and waterfowl-shaped animal haniwa, in addition to house-shaped figurines and other symbolic haniwa. Analysis of their placement suggests that symbolic haniwa were concentrated along the northern middle section, extending from the front part of the keyhole-shaped mound to the rear circular portion, and were likely confined to specific sections of the mound. ### Human-shaped Haniwa Fifteen human haniwa were identified from Kofun No. 5, including figures of armored warriors standing with both legs, male figures standing with both legs, seated female figures, and women carrying jars on their heads. Of these, 14 haniwa were classified by gender, with 9 males and 5 females, making them the oldest known human haniwa in Chiba Prefecture. While these haniwa are somewhat rough in craftsmanship, their details—particularly the horned helmets, armor, and hair styles—demonstrate considerable fidelity to actual objects. Notably, the standing warrior and male figures are unique in that their feet are directly attached to the mound without a pedestal, a feature rarely seen elsewhere. Similar examples include the wrestler haniwa from Harayama Kofun No. 1 in {{ill|Izumizaki Village|ja|泉崎村}}, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture, housed in the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Museum|ja|福島県立博物館}}. These rare artifacts were designated as town tangible cultural properties on April 1, 2018.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/419372 Cultural Heritage Database] == Construction Period == The burial goods from Kofun No. 1 suggest a 5th-century construction date, while the haniwa from Kofun No. 5 indicate a period from the late 5th century to the early 6th century. Kofun No. 4 yielded {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} suggesting a late 7th-century to early 8th-century date, possibly associated with secondary burials. While only five of the 18 mounds have been excavated, the group reflects construction over an extended period, spanning from the 5th to the 8th century. == References == * {{ill|Hajime Otsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, Saburo Kobayashi, and Masaya Kumano (Editors), ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place Names (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 * {{ill|Reiji Harashima|ja|原島礼二}}, Ryoichi Kanaizuka (Editors), ''Eastern Japan and the Yamato Court,'' Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 1994. ISBN 4-642-02186-8, p. 141, "Human-shaped Haniwa of Ogawadai Kofun No. 5" == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-4.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibit on Ogawadai Kofun No. 5] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient Japanese burial mounds in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:小川台古墳群]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]]

Kofun Group|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=20|lon_deg=140|lon_min=29|lon_sec=40|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|41|20|N|140|29|40|E}}}} }} '''Ogawadai Kofun Group''' (小川台古墳群) is a cluster of ancient burial mounds (kofun) located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Ogawadai Kofun Group is situated on a tongue-shaped plateau along the eastern midstream of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, in the central part of the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十九里浜}} region. It consists of 5 keyhole-shaped tumuli, 12 round tumuli, and 1 square tumulus. Among these, Kofun No. 1 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 28 meters), Kofun No. 2 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 21 meters), Kofun No. 3 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 24 meters), Kofun No. 4 (a square tumulus with a side length of approximately 14 meters), and Kofun No. 5 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 30 meters) were excavated in 1974. The largest mound in the group, Kofun No. 6, with a visible length of 42 meters, has yet to be investigated. == Artifacts == Significant numbers of artifacts were uncovered during the excavations of Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 5. - '''Kofun No. 1:''' Three burial facilities yielded four iron swords, one iron spear, four iron arrowheads, one iron knife, ten perforated circular stone disks, 39 spindle-shaped beads, three iron arrowheads, one iron axe, and more than 151 spindle-shaped stone beads. - '''Kofun No. 5:''' Artifacts discovered in a wooden coffin at the top of the rear circular portion include five long swords, one knife, one iron arrowhead, Hyōgo chains accompanying stirrups, one agate magatama (curved jewel), and 42 glass beads. The mound and surrounding moat contained numerous cylindrical haniwa, as well as human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, chicken-shaped, and waterfowl-shaped animal haniwa, in addition to house-shaped figurines and other symbolic haniwa. Analysis of their placement suggests that symbolic haniwa were concentrated along the northern middle section, extending from the front part of the keyhole-shaped mound to the rear circular portion, and were likely confined to specific sections of the mound. === Human-shaped Haniwa Fifteen human haniwa were identified from Kofun No. 5, including figures of armored warriors standing with both legs, male figures standing with both legs, seated female figures, and women carrying jars on their heads. Of these, 14 haniwa were classified by gender, with 9 males and 5 females, making them the oldest known human haniwa in Chiba Prefecture. While these haniwa are somewhat rough in craftsmanship, their details—particularly the horned helmets, armor, and hair styles—demonstrate considerable fidelity to actual objects. Notably, the standing warrior and male figures are unique in that their feet are directly attached to the mound without a pedestal, a feature rarely seen elsewhere. Similar examples include the wrestler haniwa from Harayama Kofun No. 1 in {{ill|Izumizaki Village|ja|泉崎村}}, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture, housed in the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Museum|ja|福島県立博物館}}. These rare artifacts were designated as town tangible cultural properties on April 1, 2018.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/419372 Cultural Heritage Database] == Construction Period == The burial goods from Kofun No. 1 suggest a 5th-century construction date, while the haniwa from Kofun No. 5 indicate a period from the late 5th century to the early 6th century. Kofun No. 4 yielded {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} suggesting a late 7th-century to early 8th-century date, possibly associated with secondary burials. While only five of the 18 mounds have been excavated, the group reflects construction over an extended period, spanning from the 5th to the 8th century. == References == * {{ill|Hajime Otsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, Saburo Kobayashi, and Masaya Kumano (Editors), ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place Names (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 * {{ill|Reiji Harashima|ja|原島礼二}}, Ryoichi Kanaizuka (Editors), ''Eastern Japan and the Yamato Court,'' Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 1994. ISBN 4-642-02186-8, p. 141, "Human-shaped Haniwa of Ogawadai Kofun No. 5" == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-4.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibit on Ogawadai Kofun No. 5] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient Japanese burial mounds in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:小川台古墳群]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:hashtag replacing]]

Among these, Kofun No. 1 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 28 meters), Kofun No. 2 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 21 meters), Kofun No. 3 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 24 meters), Kofun No. 4 (a square tumulus with a side length of approximately 14 meters), and Kofun No. 5 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 30 meters) were excavated in 1974. The largest mound in the group, Kofun No. 6, with a visible length of 42 meters, has yet to be investigated. == Artifacts == Significant numbers of artifacts were uncovered during the excavations of Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 5. - '''Kofun No. 1:''' Three burial facilities yielded four iron swords, one iron spear, four iron arrowheads, one iron knife, ten perforated circular stone disks, 39 spindle-shaped beads, three iron arrowheads, one iron axe, and more than 151 spindle-shaped stone beads. - '''Kofun No. 5:''' Artifacts discovered in a wooden coffin at the top of the rear circular portion include five long swords, one knife, one iron arrowhead, Hyōgo chains accompanying stirrups, one agate magatama (curved jewel), and 42 glass beads. The mound and surrounding moat contained numerous cylindrical haniwa, as well as human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, chicken-shaped, and waterfowl-shaped animal haniwa, in addition to house-shaped figurines and other symbolic haniwa. Analysis of their placement suggests that symbolic haniwa were concentrated along the northern middle section, extending from the front part of the keyhole-shaped mound to the rear circular portion, and were likely confined to specific sections of the mound. === Human-shaped Haniwa Fifteen human haniwa were identified from Kofun No. 5, including figures of armored warriors standing with both legs, male figures standing with both legs, seated female figures, and women carrying jars on their heads. Of these, 14 haniwa were classified by gender, with 9 males and 5 females, making them the oldest known human haniwa in Chiba Prefecture. While these haniwa are somewhat rough in craftsmanship, their details—particularly the horned helmets, armor, and hair styles—demonstrate considerable fidelity to actual objects. Notably, the standing warrior and male figures are unique in that their feet are directly attached to the mound without a pedestal, a feature rarely seen elsewhere. Similar examples include the wrestler haniwa from Harayama Kofun No. 1 in {{ill|Izumizaki Village|ja|泉崎村}}, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture, housed in the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Museum|ja|福島県立博物館}}. These rare artifacts were designated as town tangible cultural properties on April 1, 2018.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/419372 Cultural Heritage Database] == Construction Period == The burial goods from Kofun No. 1 suggest a 5th-century construction date, while the haniwa from Kofun No. 5 indicate a period from the late 5th century to the early 6th century. Kofun No. 4 yielded {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} suggesting a late 7th-century to early 8th-century date, possibly associated with secondary burials. While only five of the 18 mounds have been excavated, the group reflects construction over an extended period, spanning from the 5th to the 8th century. == References == * {{ill|Hajime Otsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, Saburo Kobayashi, and Masaya Kumano (Editors), ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place Names (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 * {{ill|Reiji Harashima|ja|原島礼二}}, Ryoichi Kanaizuka (Editors), ''Eastern Japan and the Yamato Court,'' Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 1994. ISBN 4-642-02186-8, p. 141, "Human-shaped Haniwa of Ogawadai Kofun No. 5" == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-4.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibit on Ogawadai Kofun No. 5] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient Japanese burial mounds in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:小川台古墳群]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:hashtag replacing]]

While only five of the 18 mounds have been excavated, the group reflects construction over an extended period, spanning from the 5th to the 8th century. == References == * {{ill|Hajime Otsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, Saburo Kobayashi, and Masaya Kumano (Editors), ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place Names (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 * {{ill|Reiji Harashima|ja|原島礼二}}, Ryoichi Kanaizuka (Editors), ''Eastern Japan and the Yamato Court,'' Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 1994. ISBN 4-642-02186-8, p. 141, "Human-shaped Haniwa of Ogawadai Kofun No. 5" == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | Name = Ogawadai Kofun Group | image = | Location = Ogawadai, Yokoshibahikari Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|20|N|140|29|40|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | Shape = Keyhole-shaped tumuli, round tumuli, square tumuli | Size = 28 m, 30 m, 42 m | Era = 5th century – 7th century (early 8th century) | Artifacts = Haniwa, others | Historic Designation = Town-designated tangible cultural property (haniwa artifacts) [https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html=a07 "History and Traditions" Yokoshibahikari Town Homepage] | Burial Structures = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Ogawadai
Kofun Group|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=20|lon_deg=140|lon_min=29|lon_sec=40|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|41|20|N|140|29|40|E}}}} }} '''Ogawadai Kofun Group''' (小川台古墳群) is a cluster of ancient burial mounds (kofun) located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Ogawadai Kofun Group is situated on a tongue-shaped plateau along the eastern midstream of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, in the central part of the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十九里浜}} region. It consists of 5 keyhole-shaped tumuli, 12 round tumuli, and 1 square tumulus. Among these, Kofun No. 1 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 28 meters), Kofun No. 2 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 21 meters), Kofun No. 3 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 24 meters), Kofun No. 4 (a square tumulus with a side length of approximately 14 meters), and Kofun No. 5 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 30 meters) were excavated in 1974. The largest mound in the group, Kofun No. 6, with a visible length of 42 meters, has yet to be investigated. == Artifacts == Significant numbers of artifacts were uncovered during the excavations of Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 5. - '''Kofun No. 1:''' Three burial facilities yielded four iron swords, one iron spear, four iron arrowheads, one iron knife, ten perforated circular stone disks, 39 spindle-shaped beads, three iron arrowheads, one iron axe, and more than 151 spindle-shaped stone beads. - '''Kofun No. 5:''' Artifacts discovered in a wooden coffin at the top of the rear circular portion include five long swords, one knife, one iron arrowhead, Hyōgo chains accompanying stirrups, one agate magatama (curved jewel), and 42 glass beads. The mound and surrounding moat contained numerous cylindrical haniwa, as well as human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, chicken-shaped, and waterfowl-shaped animal haniwa, in addition to house-shaped figurines and other symbolic haniwa. Analysis of their placement suggests that symbolic haniwa were concentrated along the northern middle section, extending from the front part of the keyhole-shaped mound to the rear circular portion, and were likely confined to specific sections of the mound. === Human-shaped Haniwa Fifteen human haniwa were identified from Kofun No. 5, including figures of armored warriors standing with both legs, male figures standing with both legs, seated female figures, and women carrying jars on their heads. Of these, 14 haniwa were classified by gender, with 9 males and 5 females, making them the oldest known human haniwa in Chiba Prefecture. While these haniwa are somewhat rough in craftsmanship, their details—particularly the horned helmets, armor, and hair styles—demonstrate considerable fidelity to actual objects. Notably, the standing warrior and male figures are unique in that their feet are directly attached to the mound without a pedestal, a feature rarely seen elsewhere. Similar examples include the wrestler haniwa from Harayama Kofun No. 1 in {{ill|Izumizaki Village|ja|泉崎村}}, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture, housed in the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Museum|ja|福島県立博物館}}. These rare artifacts were designated as town tangible cultural properties on April 1, 2018.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/419372 Cultural Heritage Database] == Construction Period == The burial goods from Kofun No. 1 suggest a 5th-century construction date, while the haniwa from Kofun No. 5 indicate a period from the late 5th century to the early 6th century. Kofun No. 4 yielded {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} suggesting a late 7th-century to early 8th-century date, possibly associated with secondary burials. While only five of the 18 mounds have been excavated, the group reflects construction over an extended period, spanning from the 5th to the 8th century. == References == * {{ill|Hajime Otsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, Saburo Kobayashi, and Masaya Kumano (Editors), ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place Names (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 * {{ill|Reiji Harashima|ja|原島礼二}}, Ryoichi Kanaizuka (Editors), ''Eastern Japan and the Yamato Court,'' Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 1994. ISBN 4-642-02186-8, p. 141, "Human-shaped Haniwa of Ogawadai Kofun No. 5" == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | Name = Ogawadai Kofun Group | image = | Location = Ogawadai, Yokoshibahikari Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|20|N|140|29|40|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | Shape = Keyhole-shaped tumuli, round tumuli, square tumuli | Size = 28 m, 30 m, 42 m | Era = 5th century – 7th century (early 8th century) | Artifacts = Haniwa, others | Historic Designation = Town-designated tangible cultural property (haniwa artifacts) [https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html=a07 "History and Traditions" Yokoshibahikari Town Homepage] | Burial Structures = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Ogawadai
Kofun Group|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=20|lon_deg=140|lon_min=29|lon_sec=40|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|41|20|N|140|29|40|E}}}} }} '''Ogawadai Kofun Group''' (小川台古墳群) is a cluster of ancient burial mounds (kofun) located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Ogawadai Kofun Group is situated on a tongue-shaped plateau along the eastern midstream of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, in the central part of the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十九里浜}} region. It consists of 5 keyhole-shaped tumuli, 12 round tumuli, and 1 square tumulus. Among these, Kofun No. 1 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 28 meters), Kofun No. 2 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 21 meters), Kofun No. 3 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 24 meters), Kofun No. 4 (a square tumulus with a side length of approximately 14 meters), and Kofun No. 5 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 30 meters) were excavated in 1974. The largest mound in the group, Kofun No. 6, with a visible length of 42 meters, has yet to be investigated. == Artifacts == Significant numbers of artifacts were uncovered during the excavations of Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 5. - '''Kofun No. 1:''' Three burial facilities yielded four iron swords, one iron spear, four iron arrowheads, one iron knife, ten perforated circular stone disks, 39 spindle-shaped beads, three iron arrowheads, one iron axe, and more than 151 spindle-shaped stone beads. - '''Kofun No. 5:''' Artifacts discovered in a wooden coffin at the top of the rear circular portion include five long swords, one knife, one iron arrowhead, Hyōgo chains accompanying stirrups, one agate magatama (curved jewel), and 42 glass beads. The mound and surrounding moat contained numerous cylindrical haniwa, as well as human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, chicken-shaped, and waterfowl-shaped animal haniwa, in addition to house-shaped figurines and other symbolic haniwa. Analysis of their placement suggests that symbolic haniwa were concentrated along the northern middle section, extending from the front part of the keyhole-shaped mound to the rear circular portion, and were likely confined to specific sections of the mound. === Human-shaped Haniwa Fifteen human haniwa were identified from Kofun No. 5, including figures of armored warriors standing with both legs, male figures standing with both legs, seated female figures, and women carrying jars on their heads. Of these, 14 haniwa were classified by gender, with 9 males and 5 females, making them the oldest known human haniwa in Chiba Prefecture. While these haniwa are somewhat rough in craftsmanship, their details—particularly the horned helmets, armor, and hair styles—demonstrate considerable fidelity to actual objects. Notably, the standing warrior and male figures are unique in that their feet are directly attached to the mound without a pedestal, a feature rarely seen elsewhere. Similar examples include the wrestler haniwa from Harayama Kofun No. 1 in {{ill|Izumizaki Village|ja|泉崎村}}, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture, housed in the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Museum|ja|福島県立博物館}}. These rare artifacts were designated as town tangible cultural properties on April 1, 2018.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/419372 Cultural Heritage Database] == Construction Period == The burial goods from Kofun No. 1 suggest a 5th-century construction date, while the haniwa from Kofun No. 5 indicate a period from the late 5th century to the early 6th century. Kofun No. 4 yielded {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} suggesting a late 7th-century to early 8th-century date, possibly associated with secondary burials. While only five of the 18 mounds have been excavated, the group reflects construction over an extended period, spanning from the 5th to the 8th century. == References == * {{ill|Hajime Otsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, Saburo Kobayashi, and Masaya Kumano (Editors), ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place Names (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 * {{ill|Reiji Harashima|ja|原島礼二}}, Ryoichi Kanaizuka (Editors), ''Eastern Japan and the Yamato Court,'' Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 1994. ISBN 4-642-02186-8, p. 141, "Human-shaped Haniwa of Ogawadai Kofun No. 5" == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * The mound and surrounding moat contained numerous cylindrical haniwa, as well as human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, chicken-shaped, and waterfowl-shaped animal haniwa, in addition to house-shaped figurines and other symbolic haniwa. Analysis of their placement suggests that symbolic haniwa were concentrated along the northern middle section, extending from the front part of the keyhole-shaped mound to the rear circular portion, and were likely confined to specific sections of the mound. === Human-shaped Haniwa Fifteen human haniwa were identified from Kofun No. 5, including figures of armored warriors standing with both legs, male figures standing with both legs, seated female figures, and women carrying jars on their heads. Of these, 14 haniwa were classified by gender, with 9 males and 5 females, making them the oldest known human haniwa in Chiba Prefecture. While these haniwa are somewhat rough in craftsmanship, their details—particularly the horned helmets, armor, and hair styles—demonstrate considerable fidelity to actual objects. Notably, the standing warrior and male figures are unique in that their feet are directly attached to the mound without a pedestal, a feature rarely seen elsewhere. Similar examples include the wrestler haniwa from Harayama Kofun No. 1 in {{ill|Izumizaki Village|ja|泉崎村}}, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture, housed in the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Museum|ja|福島県立博物館}}. These rare artifacts were designated as town tangible cultural properties on April 1, 2018.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/419372 Cultural Heritage Database] == Construction Period == The burial goods from Kofun No. 1 suggest a 5th-century construction date, while the haniwa from Kofun No. 5 indicate a period from the late 5th century to the early 6th century. Kofun No. 4 yielded {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} suggesting a late 7th-century to early 8th-century date, possibly associated with secondary burials. While only five of the 18 mounds have been excavated, the group reflects construction over an extended period, spanning from the 5th to the 8th century. == References == * {{ill|Hajime Otsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, Saburo Kobayashi, and Masaya Kumano (Editors), ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place Names (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 * {{ill|Reiji Harashima|ja|原島礼二}}, Ryoichi Kanaizuka (Editors), ''Eastern Japan and the Yamato Court,'' Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 1994. ISBN 4-642-02186-8, p. 141, "Human-shaped Haniwa of Ogawadai Kofun No. 5" == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-4.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibit on Ogawadai Kofun No. 5] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient Japanese burial mounds in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:小川台古墳群]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:hashtag replacing]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]]

ISBN 4-642-02186-8, p. 141, "Human-shaped Haniwa of Ogawadai Kofun No. 5" == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-4.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibit on Ogawadai Kofun No. 5] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient Japanese burial mounds in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:小川台古墳群]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:hashtag replacing]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogawadai Kofun Group}}

{{Infobox Japanese Kofun | Name = Ogawadai Kofun Group | image = | Location = Ogawadai, Yokoshibahikari Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|20|N|140|29|40|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | Shape = Keyhole-shaped tumuli, round tumuli, square tumuli | Size = 28 m, 30 m, 42 m | Era = 5th century – 7th century (early 8th century) | Artifacts = Haniwa, others | Historic Designation = Town-designated tangible cultural property (haniwa artifacts) [https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html=a07 "History and Traditions" Yokoshibahikari Town Homepage] | Burial Structures = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Ogawadai
Kofun Group|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=20|lon_deg=140|lon_min=29|lon_sec=40|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|41|20|N|140|29|40|E}}}} }} '''Ogawadai Kofun Group''' (小川台古墳群) is a cluster of ancient burial mounds (kofun) located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Ogawadai Kofun Group is situated on a tongue-shaped plateau along the eastern midstream of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, in the central part of the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十九里浜}} region. It consists of 5 keyhole-shaped tumuli, 12 round tumuli, and 1 square tumulus. Among these, Kofun No. 1 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 28 meters), Kofun No. 2 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 21 meters), Kofun No. 3 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 24 meters), Kofun No. 4 (a square tumulus with a side length of approximately 14 meters), and Kofun No. 5 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 30 meters) were excavated in 1974. The largest mound in the group, Kofun No. 6, with a visible length of 42 meters, has yet to be investigated. == Artifacts == Significant numbers of artifacts were uncovered during the excavations of Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 5. - '''Kofun No. 1:''' Three burial facilities yielded four iron swords, one iron spear, four iron arrowheads, one iron knife, ten perforated circular stone disks, 39 spindle-shaped beads, three iron arrowheads, one iron axe, and more than 151 spindle-shaped stone beads. - '''Kofun No. 5:''' Artifacts discovered in a wooden coffin at the top of the rear circular portion include five long swords, one knife, one iron arrowhead, Hyōgo chains accompanying stirrups, one agate magatama (curved jewel), and 42 glass beads. The mound and surrounding moat contained numerous cylindrical haniwa, as well as human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, chicken-shaped, and waterfowl-shaped animal haniwa, in addition to house-shaped figurines and other symbolic haniwa. Analysis of their placement suggests that symbolic haniwa were concentrated along the northern middle section, extending from the front part of the keyhole-shaped mound to the rear circular portion, and were likely confined to specific sections of the mound. === Human-shaped Haniwa Fifteen human haniwa were identified from Kofun No. 5, including figures of armored warriors standing with both legs, male figures standing with both legs, seated female figures, and women carrying jars on their heads. Of these, 14 haniwa were classified by gender, with 9 males and 5 females, making them the oldest known human haniwa in Chiba Prefecture. While these haniwa are somewhat rough in craftsmanship, their details—particularly the horned helmets, armor, and hair styles—demonstrate considerable fidelity to actual objects. Notably, the standing warrior and male figures are unique in that their feet are directly attached to the mound without a pedestal, a feature rarely seen elsewhere. Similar examples include the wrestler haniwa from Harayama Kofun No. 1 in {{ill|Izumizaki Village|ja|泉崎村}}, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture, housed in the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Museum|ja|福島県立博物館}}. These rare artifacts were designated as town tangible cultural properties on April 1, 2018.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/419372 Cultural Heritage Database] == Construction Period == The burial goods from Kofun No. 1 suggest a 5th-century construction date, while the haniwa from Kofun No. 5 indicate a period from the late 5th century to the early 6th century. Kofun No. 4 yielded {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} suggesting a late 7th-century to early 8th-century date, possibly associated with secondary burials. While only five of the 18 mounds have been excavated, the group reflects construction over an extended period, spanning from the 5th to the 8th century. == References == * {{ill|Hajime Otsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, Saburo Kobayashi, and Masaya Kumano (Editors), ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place Names (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 * {{ill|Reiji Harashima|ja|原島礼二}}, Ryoichi Kanaizuka (Editors), ''Eastern Japan and the Yamato Court,'' Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 1994. ISBN 4-642-02186-8, p. 141, "Human-shaped Haniwa of Ogawadai Kofun No. 5" == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-4.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibit on Ogawadai Kofun No. 5] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient Japanese burial mounds in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:小川台古墳群]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:hashtag replacing]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogawadai Kofun Group}}

Kofun Group|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=20|lon_deg=140|lon_min=29|lon_sec=40|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|41|20|N|140|29|40|E}}}} }} '''Ogawadai Kofun Group''' (小川台古墳群) is a cluster of ancient burial mounds (kofun) located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Ogawadai Kofun Group is situated on a tongue-shaped plateau along the eastern midstream of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, in the central part of the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十九里浜}} region. It consists of 5 keyhole-shaped tumuli, 12 round tumuli, and 1 square tumulus. Among these, Kofun No. 1 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 28 meters), Kofun No. 2 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 21 meters), Kofun No. 3 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 24 meters), Kofun No. 4 (a square tumulus with a side length of approximately 14 meters), and Kofun No. 5 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 30 meters) were excavated in 1974. The largest mound in the group, Kofun No. 6, with a visible length of 42 meters, has yet to be investigated. == Artifacts == Significant numbers of artifacts were uncovered during the excavations of Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 5. - '''Kofun No. 1:''' Three burial facilities yielded four iron swords, one iron spear, four iron arrowheads, one iron knife, ten perforated circular stone disks, 39 spindle-shaped beads, three iron arrowheads, one iron axe, and more than 151 spindle-shaped stone beads. - '''Kofun No. 5:''' Artifacts discovered in a wooden coffin at the top of the rear circular portion include five long swords, one knife, one iron arrowhead, Hyōgo chains accompanying stirrups, one agate magatama (curved jewel), and 42 glass beads. The mound and surrounding moat contained numerous cylindrical haniwa, as well as human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, chicken-shaped, and waterfowl-shaped animal haniwa, in addition to house-shaped figurines and other symbolic haniwa. Analysis of their placement suggests that symbolic haniwa were concentrated along the northern middle section, extending from the front part of the keyhole-shaped mound to the rear circular portion, and were likely confined to specific sections of the mound. === Human-shaped Haniwa Fifteen human haniwa were identified from Kofun No. 5, including figures of armored warriors standing with both legs, male figures standing with both legs, seated female figures, and women carrying jars on their heads. Of these, 14 haniwa were classified by gender, with 9 males and 5 females, making them the oldest known human haniwa in Chiba Prefecture. While these haniwa are somewhat rough in craftsmanship, their details—particularly the horned helmets, armor, and hair styles—demonstrate considerable fidelity to actual objects. Notably, the standing warrior and male figures are unique in that their feet are directly attached to the mound without a pedestal, a feature rarely seen elsewhere. Similar examples include the wrestler haniwa from Harayama Kofun No. 1 in {{ill|Izumizaki Village|ja|泉崎村}}, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture, housed in the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Museum|ja|福島県立博物館}}. These rare artifacts were designated as town tangible cultural properties on April 1, 2018.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/419372 Cultural Heritage Database] == Construction Period == The burial goods from Kofun No. 1 suggest a 5th-century construction date, while the haniwa from Kofun No. 5 indicate a period from the late 5th century to the early 6th century. Kofun No. 4 yielded {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} suggesting a late 7th-century to early 8th-century date, possibly associated with secondary burials. While only five of the 18 mounds have been excavated, the group reflects construction over an extended period, spanning from the 5th to the 8th century. == References == * {{ill|Hajime Otsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, Saburo Kobayashi, and Masaya Kumano (Editors), ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place Names (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 * {{ill|Reiji Harashima|ja|原島礼二}}, Ryoichi Kanaizuka (Editors), ''Eastern Japan and the Yamato Court,'' Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 1994. ISBN 4-642-02186-8, p. 141, "Human-shaped Haniwa of Ogawadai Kofun No. 5" == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-4.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibit on Ogawadai Kofun No. 5] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient Japanese burial mounds in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]] [[Category:SuQqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:小川台古墳群]] [[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:SuQqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:hashtag replacing]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogawadai Kofun Group}}

ISBN 4-642-02186-8, p. 141, "Human-shaped Haniwa of Ogawadai Kofun No. 5" == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-4.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibit on Ogawadai Kofun No. 5] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient Japanese Kofuns in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]] [[Category:SuQqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:小川台古墳群]] [[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:SuQqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:hashtag replacing]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogawadai Kofun Group}}

While these haniwa are somewhat rough in craftsmanship, their details—particularly the horned helmets, armor, and hair styles—demonstrate considerable fidelity to actual objects. Notably, the standing warrior and male figures are unique in that their feet are directly attached to the mound without a pedestal, a feature rarely seen elsewhere. Similar examples include the wrestler haniwa from Harayama Kofun No. 1 in {{ill|Izumizaki Village|ja|泉崎村}}, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture, housed in the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Museum|ja|福島県立博物館}}. These rare artifacts were designated as town tangible cultural properties on April 1, 2018.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/419372 Cultural Heritage Database] == Construction Period == The burial goods from Kofun No. 1 suggest a 5th-century construction date, while the haniwa from Kofun No. 5 indicate a period from the late 5th century to the early 6th century. Kofun No. 4 yielded {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} suggesting a late 7th-century to early 8th-century date, possibly associated with secondary burials. While only five of the 18 mounds have been excavated, the group reflects construction over an extended period, spanning from the 5th to the 8th century. == References == * {{ill|Hajime Otsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, Saburo Kobayashi, and Masaya Kumano (Editors), ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place Names (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 * {{ill|Reiji Harashima|ja|原島礼二}}, Ryoichi Kanaizuka (Editors), ''Eastern Japan and the Yamato Court,'' Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 1994. ISBN 4-642-02186-8, p. 141, "Human-shaped Haniwa of Ogawadai Kofun No. 5" == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | Name = Ogawadai Kofun Group | image = | Location = Ogawadai, Yokoshibahikari Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|20|N|140|29|40|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | Shape = Keyhole-shaped tumuli, round tumuli, square tumuli | Size = 28 m, 30 m, 42 m | Era = 5th century – 7th century (early 8th century) | Artifacts = Haniwa, others | Historic Designation = Town-designated tangible cultural property (haniwa artifacts) [https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html=a07 "History and Traditions" Yokoshibahikari Town Homepage] | Burial Structures = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Ogawadai
Kofun Group|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=20|lon_deg=140|lon_min=29|lon_sec=40|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|41|20|N|140|29|40|E}}}} }} '''Ogawadai Kofun Group''' (小川台古墳群) is a cluster of ancient Kofuns (kofun) located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Ogawadai Kofun Group is situated on a tongue-shaped plateau along the eastern midstream of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, in the central part of the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十九里浜}} region. It consists of 5 keyhole-shaped tumuli, 12 round tumuli, and 1 square tumulus. Among these, Kofun No. 1 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 28 meters), Kofun No. 2 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 21 meters), Kofun No. 3 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 24 meters), Kofun No. 4 (a square tumulus with a side length of approximately 14 meters), and Kofun No. 5 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 30 meters) were excavated in 1974. The largest mound in the group, Kofun No. 6, with a visible length of 42 meters, has yet to be investigated. == Artifacts == Significant numbers of artifacts were uncovered during the excavations of Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 5. - '''Kofun No. 1:''' Three burial facilities yielded four iron swords, one iron spear, four iron arrowheads, one iron knife, ten perforated circular stone disks, 39 spindle-shaped beads, three iron arrowheads, one iron axe, and more than 151 spindle-shaped stone beads. - '''Kofun No. 5:''' Artifacts discovered in a wooden coffin at the top of the rear circular portion include five long swords, one knife, one iron arrowhead, Hyōgo chains accompanying stirrups, one agate magatama (curved jewel), and 42 glass beads. The mound and surrounding moat contained numerous cylindrical haniwa, as well as human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, chicken-shaped, and waterfowl-shaped animal haniwa, in addition to house-shaped figurines and other symbolic haniwa. Analysis of their placement suggests that symbolic haniwa were concentrated along the northern middle section, extending from the front part of the keyhole-shaped mound to the rear circular portion, and were likely confined to specific sections of the mound. === Human-shaped Haniwa Fifteen human haniwa were identified from Kofun No. 5, including figures of armored warriors standing with both legs, male figures standing with both legs, seated female figures, and women carrying jars on their heads. Of these, 14 haniwa were classified by gender, with 9 males and 5 females, making them the oldest known human haniwa in Chiba Prefecture. While these haniwa are somewhat rough in craftsmanship, their details—particularly the horned helmets, armor, and hair styles—demonstrate considerable fidelity to actual objects. Notably, the standing warrior and male figures are unique in that their feet are directly attached to the mound without a pedestal, a feature rarely seen elsewhere. Similar examples include the wrestler haniwa from Harayama Kofun No. 1 in {{ill|Izumizaki Village|ja|泉崎村}}, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture, housed in the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Museum|ja|福島県立博物館}}. These rare artifacts were designated as town tangible cultural properties on April 1, 2018.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/419372 Cultural Heritage Database] == Construction Period == The burial goods from Kofun No. 1 suggest a 5th-century construction date, while the haniwa from Kofun No. 5 indicate a period from the late 5th century to the early 6th century. Kofun No. 4 yielded {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} suggesting a late 7th-century to early 8th-century date, possibly associated with secondary burials. While only five of the 18 mounds have been excavated, the group reflects construction over an extended period, spanning from the 5th to the 8th century. == References == * {{ill|Hajime Otsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, Saburo Kobayashi, and Masaya Kumano (Editors), ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place Names (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 * {{ill|Reiji Harashima|ja|原島礼二}}, Ryoichi Kanaizuka (Editors), ''Eastern Japan and the Yamato Court,'' Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 1994. ISBN 4-642-02186-8, p. 141, "Human-shaped Haniwa of Ogawadai Kofun No. 5" == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == *
