{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | Name = Ogawadai Kofun Group | image = | Location = Ogawadai, Yokoshibahikari Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|20|N|140|29|40|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumuli, round tumuli, square tumuli | area = 28 m, 30 m, 42 m | Era = 5th century – 7th century (early 8th century) | Artifacts = Haniwa, others | Historic Designation = Town-designated tangible cultural property (haniwa artifacts) [https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html=a07 "History and Traditions" Yokoshibahikari Town Homepage] | Burial Structures = | map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Ogawadai
Kofun Group|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=20|lon_deg=140|lon_min=29|lon_sec=40|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|41|20|N|140|29|40|E}}}} }} '''Ogawadai Kofun Group''' (小川台古墳群) is a cluster of ancient Kofuns (kofun) located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Ogawadai Kofun Group is situated on a tongue-shaped plateau along the eastern midstream of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, in the central part of the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十九里浜}} region. It consists of 5 keyhole-shaped tumuli, 12 round tumuli, and 1 square tumulus. Among these, Kofun No. 1 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 28 meters), Kofun No. 2 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 21 meters), Kofun No. 3 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 24 meters), Kofun No. 4 (a square tumulus with a side length of approximately 14 meters), and Kofun No. 5 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 30 meters) were excavated in 1974. The largest mound in the group, Kofun No. 6, with a visible length of 42 meters, has yet to be investigated. == Artifacts == Significant numbers of artifacts were uncovered during the excavations of Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 5. * '''Kofun No. 1:''' Three burial facilities yielded four iron swords, one iron spear, four iron arrowheads, one iron knife, ten perforated circular stone disks, 39 spindle-shaped beads, three iron arrowheads, one iron axe, and more than 151 spindle-shaped stone beads. * '''Kofun No. 5:''' Artifacts discovered in a wooden coffin at the top of the rear circular portion include five long swords, one knife, one iron arrowhead, Hyōgo chains accompanying stirrups, one agate magatama (curved jewel), and 42 glass beads. The mound and surrounding moat contained numerous cylindrical haniwa, as well as human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, chicken-shaped, and waterfowl-shaped animal haniwa, in addition to house-shaped figurines and other symbolic haniwa. Analysis of their placement suggests that symbolic haniwa were concentrated along the northern middle section, extending from the front part of the keyhole-shaped mound to the rear circular portion, and were likely confined to specific sections of the mound. === Human-shaped Haniwa Fifteen human haniwa were identified from Kofun No. 5, including figures of armored warriors standing with both legs, male figures standing with both legs, seated female figures, and women carrying jars on their heads. Of these, 14 haniwa were classified by gender, with 9 males and 5 females, making them the oldest known human haniwa in Chiba Prefecture. While these haniwa are somewhat rough in craftsmanship, their details—particularly the horned helmets, armor, and hair styles—demonstrate considerable fidelity to actual objects. Notably, the standing warrior and male figures are unique in that their feet are directly attached to the mound without a pedestal, a feature rarely seen elsewhere. Similar examples include the wrestler haniwa from Harayama Kofun No. 1 in {{ill|Izumizaki Village|ja|泉崎村}}, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture, housed in the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Museum|ja|福島県立博物館}}. These rare artifacts were designated as town tangible cultural properties on April 1, 2018.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/419372 other_designation Database] == built == The burial goods from Kofun No. 1 suggest a 5th-century construction date, while the haniwa from Kofun No. 5 indicate a period from the late 5th century to the early 6th century. Kofun No. 4 yielded {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} suggesting a late 7th-century to early 8th-century date, possibly associated with secondary burials. While only five of the 18 mounds have been excavated, the group reflects construction over an extended period, spanning from the 5th to the 8th century. == References == * {{ill|Hajime Otsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, Saburo Kobayashi, and Masaya Kumano (Editors), ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place Names (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 * {{ill|Reiji Harashima|ja|原島礼二}}, Ryoichi Kanaizuka (Editors), ''Eastern Japan and the Yamato Court,'' Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 1994. ISBN 4-642-02186-8, p. 141, "Human-shaped Haniwa of Ogawadai Kofun No. 5" == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-4.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibit on Ogawadai Kofun No. 5] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Ancient Japanese Kofuns in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:小川台古墳群]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogawadai Kofun Group}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogawadai Kofun Group}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogawadai Kofun Group}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | Name = Ogawadai Kofun Group | image = | Location = Ogawadai, Yokoshibahikari Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|20|N|140|29|40|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumuli, round tumuli, square tumuli | area = 28 m, 30 m, 42 m | Era = 5th century – 7th century (early 8th century) | Artifacts = Haniwa, others | Historic Designation = Town-designated tangible cultural property (haniwa artifacts) [https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html=a07 "History and Traditions" Yokoshibahikari Town Homepage] | Burial Structures = | map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Ogawadai
Kofun Group|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=20|lon_deg=140|lon_min=29|lon_sec=40|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|41|20|N|140|29|40|E}}}} }} '''Ogawadai Kofun Group''' (小川台古墳群) is a cluster of ancient Kofuns (kofun) located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Ogawadai Kofun Group is situated on a tongue-shaped plateau along the eastern midstream of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, in the central part of the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十九里浜}} region. It consists of 5 keyhole-shaped tumuli, 12 round tumuli, and 1 square tumulus. Among these, Kofun No. 1 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 28 meters), Kofun No. 2 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 21 meters), Kofun No. 3 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 24 meters), Kofun No. 4 (a square tumulus with a side length of approximately 14 meters), and Kofun No. 5 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 30 meters) were excavated in 1974. The largest mound in the group, Kofun No. 6, with a visible length of 42 meters, has yet to be investigated. == Artifacts == Significant numbers of artifacts were uncovered during the excavations of Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 5. * '''Kofun No. 1:''' Three burial facilities yielded four iron swords, one iron spear, four iron arrowheads, one iron knife, ten perforated circular stone disks, 39 spindle-shaped beads, three iron arrowheads, one iron axe, and more than 151 spindle-shaped stone beads. * '''Kofun No. 5:''' Artifacts discovered in a wooden coffin at the top of the rear circular portion include five long swords, one knife, one iron arrowhead, Hyōgo chains accompanying stirrups, one agate magatama (curved jewel), and 42 glass beads. The mound and surrounding moat contained numerous cylindrical haniwa, as well as human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, chicken-shaped, and waterfowl-shaped animal haniwa, in addition to house-shaped figurines and other symbolic haniwa. Analysis of their placement suggests that symbolic haniwa were concentrated along the northern middle section, extending from the front part of the keyhole-shaped mound to the rear circular portion, and were likely confined to specific sections of the mound. === Human-shaped Haniwa Fifteen human haniwa were identified from Kofun No. 5, including figures of armored warriors standing with both legs, male figures standing with both legs, seated female figures, and women carrying jars on their heads. Of these, 14 haniwa were classified by gender, with 9 males and 5 females, making them the oldest known human haniwa in Chiba Prefecture. While these haniwa are somewhat rough in craftsmanship, their details—particularly the horned helmets, armor, and hair styles—demonstrate considerable fidelity to actual objects. Notably, the standing warrior and male figures are unique in that their feet are directly attached to the mound without a pedestal, a feature rarely seen elsewhere. Similar examples include the wrestler haniwa from Harayama Kofun No. 1 in {{ill|Izumizaki Village|ja|泉崎村}}, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture, housed in the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Museum|ja|福島県立博物館}}. These rare artifacts were designated as town tangible cultural properties on April 1, 2018.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/419372 other_designation Database] == built == The burial goods from Kofun No. 1 suggest a 5th-century construction date, while the haniwa from Kofun No. 5 indicate a period from the late 5th century to the early 6th century. Kofun No. 4 yielded {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} suggesting a late 7th-century to early 8th-century date, possibly associated with secondary burials. While only five of the 18 mounds have been excavated, the group reflects construction over an extended period, spanning from the 5th to the 8th century. == References == * {{ill|Hajime Otsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, Saburo Kobayashi, and Masaya Kumano (Editors), ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place Names (Japanese Historical Place Names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 * {{ill|Reiji Harashima|ja|原島礼二}}, Ryoichi Kanaizuka (Editors), ''Eastern Japan and the Yamato Court,'' Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 1994. ISBN 4-642-02186-8, p. 141, "Human-shaped Haniwa of Ogawadai Kofun No. 5" == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-4.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibit on Ogawadai Kofun No. 5] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Ancient Japanese Kofuns in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:小川台古墳群]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogawadai Kofun Group}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogawadai Kofun Group}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogawadai Kofun Group}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:bump]]

While only five of the 18 mounds have been excavated, the group reflects construction over an extended period, spanning from the 5th to the 8th century. == References == * {{ill|Hajime Otsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, Saburo Kobayashi, and Masaya Kumano (Editors), ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Ogawadai Kofun Group | image = | location = Ogawadai, Yokoshibahikari Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|20|N|140|29|40|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumuli, round tumuli, square tumuli | area = 28 m, 30 m, 42 m | Era = 5th century – 7th century (early 8th century) | Artifacts = Haniwa, others | Historic Designation = Town-designated tangible cultural property (haniwa artifacts) [https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html=a07 "History and Traditions" Yokoshibahikari Town Homepage] | Burial Structures = | map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Ogawadai
Kofun Group|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=20|lon_deg=140|lon_min=29|lon_sec=40|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|41|20|N|140|29|40|E}}}} }} '''Ogawadai Kofun Group''' (小川台古墳群) is a cluster of ancient Kofuns (kofun) located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Ogawadai Kofun Group is situated on a tongue-shaped plateau along the eastern midstream of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, in the central part of the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十九里浜}} region. It consists of 5 keyhole-shaped tumuli, 12 round tumuli, and 1 square tumulus. Among these, Kofun No. 1 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 28 meters), Kofun No. 2 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 21 meters), Kofun No. 3 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 24 meters), Kofun No. 4 (a square tumulus with a side length of approximately 14 meters), and Kofun No. 5 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 30 meters) were excavated in 1974. The largest mound in the group, Kofun No. 6, with a visible length of 42 meters, has yet to be investigated. == Artifacts == Significant numbers of artifacts were uncovered during the excavations of Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 5. * '''Kofun No. 1:''' Three burial facilities yielded four iron swords, one iron spear, four iron arrowheads, one iron knife, ten perforated circular stone disks, 39 spindle-shaped beads, three iron arrowheads, one iron axe, and more than 151 spindle-shaped stone beads. * '''Kofun No. 5:''' Artifacts discovered in a wooden coffin at the top of the rear circular portion include five long swords, one knife, one iron arrowhead, Hyōgo chains accompanying stirrups, one agate magatama (curved jewel), and 42 glass beads. The mound and surrounding moat contained numerous cylindrical haniwa, as well as human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, chicken-shaped, and waterfowl-shaped animal haniwa, in addition to house-shaped figurines and other symbolic haniwa. Analysis of their placement suggests that symbolic haniwa were concentrated along the northern middle section, extending from the front part of the keyhole-shaped mound to the rear circular portion, and were likely confined to specific sections of the mound. === Human-shaped Haniwa Fifteen human haniwa were identified from Kofun No. 5, including figures of armored warriors standing with both legs, male figures standing with both legs, seated female figures, and women carrying jars on their heads. Of these, 14 haniwa were classified by gender, with 9 males and 5 females, making them the oldest known human haniwa in Chiba Prefecture. While these haniwa are somewhat rough in craftsmanship, their details—particularly the horned helmets, armor, and hair styles—demonstrate considerable fidelity to actual objects. Notably, the standing warrior and male figures are unique in that their feet are directly attached to the mound without a pedestal, a feature rarely seen elsewhere. Similar examples include the wrestler haniwa from Harayama Kofun No. 1 in {{ill|Izumizaki Village|ja|泉崎村}}, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture, housed in the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Museum|ja|福島県立博物館}}. These rare artifacts were designated as town tangible cultural properties on April 1, 2018.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/419372 other_designation Database] == built == The burial goods from Kofun No. 1 suggest a 5th-century construction date, while the haniwa from Kofun No. 5 indicate a period from the late 5th century to the early 6th century. Kofun No. 4 yielded {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} suggesting a late 7th-century to early 8th-century date, possibly associated with secondary burials. While only five of the 18 mounds have been excavated, the group reflects construction over an extended period, spanning from the 5th to the 8th century. == References == * {{ill|Hajime Otsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, Saburo Kobayashi, and Masaya Kumano (Editors), ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 * {{ill|Reiji Harashima|ja|原島礼二}}, Ryoichi Kanaizuka (Editors), ''Eastern Japan and the Yamato Court,'' Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 1994. ISBN 4-642-02186-8, p. 141, "Human-shaped Haniwa of Ogawadai Kofun No. 5" == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-4.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibit on Ogawadai Kofun No. 5] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Ancient Japanese Kofuns in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:小川台古墳群]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogawadai Kofun Group}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogawadai Kofun Group}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogawadai Kofun Group}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New2]]

Notably, the standing warrior and male figures are unique in that their feet are directly attached to the mound without a pedestal, a feature rarely seen elsewhere. These rare artifacts were designated as town tangible cultural properties on April 1, 2018.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/419372 other_designation Database] == built == The burial goods from Kofun No. 1 suggest a 5th-century construction date, while the haniwa from Kofun No. 5 indicate a period from the late 5th century to the early 6th century. Kofun No. 4 yielded {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} suggesting a late 7th-century to early 8th-century date, possibly associated with secondary burials. While only five of the 18 mounds have been excavated, the group reflects construction over an extended period, spanning from the 5th to the 8th century. == References == * {{ill|Hajime Otsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, Saburo Kobayashi, and Masaya Kumano (Editors), ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 * {{ill|Reiji Harashima|ja|原島礼二}}, Ryoichi Kanaizuka (Editors), ''Eastern Japan and the Yamato Court,'' Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 1994. ISBN 4-642-02186-8, p. 141, "Human-shaped Haniwa of Ogawadai Kofun No. 5" == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-4.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibit on Ogawadai Kofun No. 5] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Ancient Japanese Kofuns in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:小川台古墳群]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogawadai Kofun Group}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogawadai Kofun Group}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogawadai Kofun Group}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Ogawadai Kofun Group | image = | location = Ogawadai, Yokoshibahikari Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|20|N|140|29|40|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumuli, round tumuli, square tumuli | area = 28 m, 30 m, 42 m | Era = 5th century – 7th century (early 8th century) | burial goods = Haniwa, others | alternate_name = Town-designation1 tangible cultural property (haniwa burial goods) [https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html=a07 "History and Traditions" Yokoshibahikari Town Homepage] | Burial Structures = | map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Ogawadai
Kofun Group|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=20|lon_deg=140|lon_min=29|lon_sec=40|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|41|20|N|140|29|40|E}}}} }} '''Ogawadai Kofun Group''' (小川台古墳群) is a cluster of ancient Kofuns (kofun) located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Ogawadai Kofun Group is situated on a tongue-shaped plateau along the eastern midstream of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, in the central part of the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十九里浜}} region. It consists of 5 keyhole-shaped tumuli, 12 round tumuli, and 1 square tumulus. Among these, Kofun No. 1 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 28 meters), Kofun No. 2 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 21 meters), Kofun No. 3 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 24 meters), Kofun No. 4 (a square tumulus with a side length of approximately 14 meters), and Kofun No. 5 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 30 meters) were excavated in 1974. The largest mound in the group, Kofun No. 6, with a visible length of 42 meters, has yet to be investigated. == burial goods == Significant numbers of burial goods were uncovered during the excavations of Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 5. * '''Kofun No. 1:''' Three burial facilities yielded four iron swords, one iron spear, four iron arrowheads, one iron knife, ten perforated circular stone disks, 39 spindle-shaped beads, three iron arrowheads, one iron axe, and more than 151 spindle-shaped stone beads. * '''Kofun No. 5:''' burial goods discovered in a wooden coffin at the top of the rear circular portion include five long swords, one knife, one iron arrowhead, Hyōgo chains accompanying stirrups, one agate magatama (curved jewel), and 42 glass beads. The mound and surrounding moat contained numerous cylindrical haniwa, as well as human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, chicken-shaped, and waterfowl-shaped animal haniwa, in addition to house-shaped figurines and other symbolic haniwa. Analysis of their placement suggests that symbolic haniwa were concentrated along the northern middle section, extending from the front part of the keyhole-shaped mound to the rear circular portion, and were likely confined to specific sections of the mound. === Human-shaped Haniwa Fifteen human haniwa were identified from Kofun No. 5, including figures of armored warriors standing with both legs, male figures standing with both legs, seated female figures, and women carrying jars on their heads. Of these, 14 haniwa were classified by gender, with 9 males and 5 females, making them the oldest known human haniwa in Chiba Prefecture. While these haniwa are somewhat rough in craftsmanship, their details—particularly the horned helmets, armor, and hair styles—demonstrate considerable fidelity to actual objects. Notably, the standing warrior and male figures are unique in that their feet are directly attached to the mound without a pedestal, a feature rarely seen elsewhere. Similar examples include the wrestler haniwa from Harayama Kofun No. 1 in {{ill|Izumizaki Village|ja|泉崎村}}, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture, housed in the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Museum|ja|福島県立博物館}}. These rare burial goods were designation1 as town tangible cultural properties on April 1, 2018.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/419372 other_designation Database] == built == The burial goods from Kofun No. 1 suggest a 5th-century construction date, while the haniwa from Kofun No. 5 indicate a period from the late 5th century to the early 6th century. Kofun No. 4 yielded {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} suggesting a late 7th-century to early 8th-century date, possibly associated with secondary burials. While only five of the 18 mounds have been excavated, the group reflects construction over an extended period, spanning from the 5th to the 8th century. == References == * {{ill|Hajime Otsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, Saburo Kobayashi, and Masaya Kumano (Editors), ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 * {{ill|Reiji Harashima|ja|原島礼二}}, Ryoichi Kanaizuka (Editors), ''Eastern Japan and the Yamato Court,'' Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 1994. ISBN 4-642-02186-8, p. 141, "Human-shaped Haniwa of Ogawadai Kofun No. 5" == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-4.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibit on Ogawadai Kofun No. 5] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Ancient Japanese Kofuns in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:小川台古墳群]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogawadai Kofun Group}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogawadai Kofun Group}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogawadai Kofun Group}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]]

Notably, the standing warrior and male figures are unique in that their feet are directly attached to the mound without a pedestal, a feature rarely seen elsewhere. Similar examples include the wrestler haniwa from Harayama Kofun No. 1 in {{ill|Izumizaki Village|ja|泉崎村}}, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture, housed in the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Museum|ja|福島県立博物館}}. These rare burial goods were other_designation1 as town tangible cultural properties on April 1, 2018.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/419372 other_other_designation Database] == built == The burial goods from Kofun No. 1 suggest a 5th-century construction date, while the haniwa from Kofun No. 5 indicate a period from the late 5th century to the early 6th century. Kofun No. 4 yielded {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} suggesting a late 7th-century to early 8th-century date, possibly associated with secondary burials. While only five of the 18 mounds have been excavated, the group reflects construction over an extended period, spanning from the 5th to the 8th century. == References == * {{ill|Hajime Otsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, Saburo Kobayashi, and Masaya Kumano (Editors), ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 * {{ill|Reiji Harashima|ja|原島礼二}}, Ryoichi Kanaizuka (Editors), ''Eastern Japan and the Yamato Court,'' Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 1994. ISBN 4-642-02186-8, p. 141, "Human-shaped Haniwa of Ogawadai Kofun No. 5" == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-4.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibit on Ogawadai Kofun No. 5] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Ancient Japanese Kofuns in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:小川台古墳群]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogawadai Kofun Group}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogawadai Kofun Group}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogawadai Kofun Group}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New2]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Ogawadai Kofun Group | image = | location = Ogawadai, Yokoshibahikari Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|20|N|140|29|40|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumuli, round tumuli, square tumuli | area = 28 m, 30 m, 42 m | Era = 5th century – 7th century (early 8th century) | burial goods = Haniwa, others | alternate_name = Town-other_designation1 tangible cultural property (haniwa burial goods) [https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html=a07 "History and Traditions" Yokoshibahikari Town Homepage] | Burial Structures = | map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Ogawadai
Kofun Group|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=20|lon_deg=140|lon_min=29|lon_sec=40|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|41|20|N|140|29|40|E}}}} }} '''Ogawadai Kofun Group''' (小川台古墳群) is a cluster of ancient Kofuns (kofun) located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Ogawadai Kofun Group is situated on a tongue-shaped plateau along the eastern midstream of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, in the central part of the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十九里浜}} region. It consists of 5 keyhole-shaped tumuli, 12 round tumuli, and 1 square tumulus. Among these, Kofun No. 1 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 28 meters), Kofun No. 2 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 21 meters), Kofun No. 3 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 24 meters), Kofun No. 4 (a square tumulus with a side length of approximately 14 meters), and Kofun No. 5 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 30 meters) were excavated in 1974. The largest mound in the group, Kofun No. 6, with a visible length of 42 meters, has yet to be investigated. == burial goods == Significant numbers of burial goods were uncovered during the excavations of Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 5. * '''Kofun No. 1:''' Three burial facilities yielded four iron swords, one iron spear, four iron arrowheads, one iron knife, ten perforated circular stone disks, 39 spindle-shaped beads, three iron arrowheads, one iron axe, and more than 151 spindle-shaped stone beads. * '''Kofun No. 5:''' burial goods discovered in a wooden coffin at the top of the rear circular portion include five long swords, one knife, one iron arrowhead, Hyōgo chains accompanying stirrups, one agate magatama (curved jewel), and 42 glass beads. The mound and surrounding moat contained numerous cylindrical haniwa, as well as human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, chicken-shaped, and waterfowl-shaped animal haniwa, in addition to house-shaped figurines and other symbolic haniwa. Analysis of their placement suggests that symbolic haniwa were concentrated along the northern middle section, extending from the front part of the keyhole-shaped mound to the rear circular portion, and were likely confined to specific sections of the mound. === Human-shaped Haniwa Fifteen human haniwa were identified from Kofun No. 5, including figures of armored warriors standing with both legs, male figures standing with both legs, seated female figures, and women carrying jars on their heads. Of these, 14 haniwa were classified by gender, with 9 males and 5 females, making them the oldest known human haniwa in Chiba Prefecture. While these haniwa are somewhat rough in craftsmanship, their details—particularly the horned helmets, armor, and hair styles—demonstrate considerable fidelity to actual objects. Notably, the standing warrior and male figures are unique in that their feet are directly attached to the mound without a pedestal, a feature rarely seen elsewhere. Similar examples include the wrestler haniwa from Harayama Kofun No. 1 in {{ill|Izumizaki Village|ja|泉崎村}}, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture, housed in the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Museum|ja|福島県立博物館}}. These rare burial goods were other_designation1 as town tangible cultural properties on April 1, 2018.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/419372 other_other_designation Database] == built == The burial goods from Kofun No. 1 suggest a 5th-century construction date, while the haniwa from Kofun No. 5 indicate a period from the late 5th century to the early 6th century. Kofun No. 4 yielded {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} suggesting a late 7th-century to early 8th-century date, possibly associated with secondary burials. While only five of the 18 mounds have been excavated, the group reflects construction over an extended period, spanning from the 5th to the 8th century. == References == * {{ill|Hajime Otsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, Saburo Kobayashi, and Masaya Kumano (Editors), ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 * {{ill|Reiji Harashima|ja|原島礼二}}, Ryoichi Kanaizuka (Editors), ''Eastern Japan and the Yamato Court,'' Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 1994. ISBN 4-642-02186-8, p. 141, "Human-shaped Haniwa of Ogawadai Kofun No. 5" == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-4.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibit on Ogawadai Kofun No. 5] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Ancient Japanese Kofuns in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:小川台古墳群]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogawadai Kofun Group}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogawadai Kofun Group}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogawadai Kofun Group}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]]

Among these, Kofun No. 1 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 28 meters), Kofun No. 2 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 21 meters), Kofun No. 3 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 24 meters), Kofun No. 4 (a square tumulus with a side length of approximately 14 meters), and Kofun No. 5 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 30 meters) were excavated in 1974. The largest mound in the group, Kofun No. 6, with a visible length of 42 meters, has yet to be investigated. == burial goods == Significant numbers of burial goods were uncovered during the excavations of Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 5. * '''Kofun No. 1:''' Three burial facilities yielded four iron swords, one iron spear, four iron arrowheads, one iron knife, ten perforated circular stone disks, 39 spindle-shaped beads, three iron arrowheads, one iron axe, and more than 151 spindle-shaped stone beads. * '''Kofun No. 5:''' burial goods discovered in a wooden coffin at the top of the rear circular portion include five long swords, one knife, one iron arrowhead, Hyōgo chains accompanying stirrups, one agate magatama (curved jewel), and 42 glass beads. The mound and surrounding moat contained numerous cylindrical haniwa, as well as human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, chicken-shaped, and waterfowl-shaped animal haniwa, in addition to house-shaped figurines and other symbolic haniwa. Analysis of their placement suggests that symbolic haniwa were concentrated along the northern middle section, extending from the front part of the keyhole-shaped mound to the rear circular portion, and were likely confined to specific sections of the mound. === Human-shaped Haniwa Fifteen human haniwa were identified from Kofun No. 5, including figures of armored warriors standing with both legs, male figures standing with both legs, seated female figures, and women carrying jars on their heads. Of these, 14 haniwa were classified by gender, with 9 males and 5 females, making them the oldest known human haniwa in Chiba Prefecture. While these haniwa are somewhat rough in craftsmanship, their details—particularly the horned helmets, armor, and hair styles—demonstrate considerable fidelity to actual objects. Notably, the standing warrior and male figures are unique in that their feet are directly attached to the mound without a pedestal, a feature rarely seen elsewhere. Similar examples include the wrestler haniwa from Harayama Kofun No. 1 in {{ill|Izumizaki Village|ja|泉崎村}}, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture, housed in the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Museum|ja|福島県立博物館}}. These rare burial goods were other_designation1 as town tangible cultural properties on April 1, 2018.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/419372 other_other_designation Database] == built == The burial goods from Kofun No. 1 suggest a 5th-century construction date, while the haniwa from Kofun No. 5 indicate a period from the late 5th century to the early 6th century. Kofun No. 4 yielded {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} suggesting a late 7th-century to early 8th-century date, possibly associated with secondary burials. While only five of the 18 mounds have been excavated, the group reflects construction over an extended period, spanning from the 5th to the 8th century. == References == * {{ill|Hajime Otsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, Saburo Kobayashi, and Masaya Kumano (Editors), ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 * {{ill|Reiji Harashima|ja|原島礼二}}, Ryoichi Kanaizuka (Editors), ''Eastern Japan and the Yamato Court,'' Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 1994. ISBN 4-642-02186-8, p. 141, "Human-shaped Haniwa of Ogawadai Kofun No. 5" == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-4.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibit on Ogawadai Kofun No. 5] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Ancient Japanese Kofuns in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Ogawadai Kofun Group]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:小川台古墳群]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogawadai Kofun Group}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogawadai Kofun Group}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogawadai Kofun Group}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ogawadai Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]]

Kofun Group|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=20|lon_deg=140|lon_min=29|lon_sec=40|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|41|20|N|140|29|40|E}}}} }} '''Ogawadai Kofun Group''' (小川台古墳群) is a cluster of ancient Kofuns (kofun) located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Ogawadai Kofun Group is situated on a tongue-shaped plateau along the eastern midstream of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, in the central part of the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十九里浜}} region. It consists of 5 keyhole-shaped tumuli, 12 round tumuli, and 1 square tumulus. Among these, Kofun No. 1 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 28 meters), Kofun No. 2 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 21 meters), Kofun No. 3 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 24 meters), Kofun No. 4 (a square tumulus with a side length of approximately 14 meters), and Kofun No. 5 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 30 meters) were excavated in 1974. The mound and surrounding moat contained numerous cylindrical haniwa, as well as human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, chicken-shaped, and waterfowl-shaped animal haniwa, in addition to house-shaped figurines and other symbolic haniwa. Analysis of their placement suggests that symbolic haniwa were concentrated along the northern middle section, extending from the front part of the keyhole-shaped mound to the rear circular portion, and were likely confined to specific sections of the mound. === Human-shaped Haniwa Fifteen human haniwa were identified from Kofun No. 5, including figures of armored warriors standing with both legs, male figures standing with both legs, seated female figures, and women carrying jars on their heads. Of these, 14 haniwa were classified by gender, with 9 males and 5 females, making them the oldest known human haniwa in Chiba Prefecture. While these haniwa are somewhat rough in craftsmanship, their details—particularly the horned helmets, armor, and hair styles—demonstrate considerable fidelity to actual objects. Notably, the standing warrior and male figures are unique in that their feet are directly attached to the mound without a pedestal, a feature rarely seen elsewhere. Similar examples include the wrestler haniwa from Harayama Kofun No. 1 in {{ill|Izumizaki Village|ja|泉崎村}}, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture, housed in the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Museum|ja|福島県立博物館}}. These rare burial goods were other_designation1 as town tangible cultural properties on April 1, 2018.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/419372 other_designation Database] == built == The burial goods from Kofun No. 1 suggest a 5th-century construction date, while the haniwa from Kofun No. 5 indicate a period from the late 5th century to the early 6th century. While only five of the 18 mounds have been excavated, the group reflects construction over an extended period, spanning from the 5th to the 8th century. == References == * {{ill|Hajime Otsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, Saburo Kobayashi, and Masaya Kumano (Editors), ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. Kofun Group|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=20|lon_deg=140|lon_min=29|lon_sec=40|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|41|20|N|140|29|40|E}}}} }} '''Ogawadai Kofun Group''' (小川台古墳群) is a cluster of ancient Kofuns (kofun) located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Ogawadai Kofun Group is situated on a tongue-shaped plateau along the eastern midstream of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, in the central part of the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十九里浜}} region. It consists of 5 keyhole-shaped tumuli, 12 round tumuli, and 1 square tumulus. Among these, Kofun No. 1 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 28 meters), Kofun No. 2 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 21 meters), Kofun No. 3 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 24 meters), Kofun No. 4 (a square tumulus with a side length of approximately 14 meters), and Kofun No. 5 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 30 meters) were excavated in 1974. The largest mound in the group, Kofun No. 6, with a visible length of 42 meters, has yet to be investigated. == burial goods == Significant numbers of burial goods were uncovered during the excavations of Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 5. * '''Kofun No. 1:''' Three burial facilities yielded four iron swords, one iron spear, four iron arrowheads, one iron knife, ten perforated circular stone disks, 39 spindle-shaped beads, three iron arrowheads, one iron axe, and more than 151 spindle-shaped stone beads. * '''Kofun No. 5:''' burial goods discovered in a wooden coffin at the top of the rear circular portion include five long swords, one knife, one iron arrowhead, Hyōgo chains accompanying stirrups, one agate magatama (curved jewel), and 42 glass beads. The mound and surrounding moat contained numerous cylindrical haniwa, as well as human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, chicken-shaped, and waterfowl-shaped animal haniwa, in addition to house-shaped figurines and other symbolic haniwa. Analysis of their placement suggests that symbolic haniwa were concentrated along the northern middle section, extending from the front part of the keyhole-shaped mound to the rear circular portion, and were likely confined to specific sections of the mound. === Human-shaped Haniwa Fifteen human haniwa were identified from Kofun No. 5, including figures of armored warriors standing with both legs, male figures standing with both legs, seated female figures, and women carrying jars on their heads. Of these, 14 haniwa were classified by gender, with 9 males and 5 females, making them the oldest known human haniwa in Chiba Prefecture. While these haniwa are somewhat rough in craftsmanship, their details—particularly the horned helmets, armor, and hair styles—demonstrate considerable fidelity to actual objects. Notably, the standing warrior and male figures are unique in that their feet are directly attached to the mound without a pedestal, a feature rarely seen elsewhere. Similar examples include the wrestler haniwa from Harayama Kofun No. 1 in {{ill|Izumizaki Village|ja|泉崎村}}, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture, housed in the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Museum|ja|福島県立博物館}}. These rare burial goods were other_designation1 as town tangible cultural properties on April 1, 2018.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/419372 other_designation Database] == built == The burial goods from Kofun No. 1 suggest a 5th-century construction date, while the haniwa from Kofun No. 5 indicate a period from the late 5th century to the early 6th century. Kofun No. 4 yielded {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} suggesting a late 7th-century to early 8th-century date, possibly associated with secondary burials. While only five of the 18 mounds have been excavated, the group reflects construction over an extended period, spanning from the 5th to the 8th century. == References == * {{ill|Hajime Otsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, Saburo Kobayashi, and Masaya Kumano (Editors), ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 * {{ill|Reiji Harashima|ja|原島礼二}}, Ryoichi Kanaizuka (Editors), ''Eastern Japan and the Yamato Court,'' Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 1994. ISBN 4-642-02186-8, p. 141, "Human-shaped Haniwa of Ogawadai Kofun No. 5" == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-4.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibit on Ogawadai Kofun No. 5] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Ancient Japanese Kofuns in Chiba Prefecture]] This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Ogawadai Kofun Group | image = | location = Ogawadai, Yokoshibahikari Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|20|N|140|29|40|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumuli, round tumuli, square tumuli | area = 28 m, 30 m, 42 m | Era = 5th century – 7th century (early 8th century) | burial goods = Haniwa, others | alternate_name = Town-other_designation1 tangible cultural property (haniwa burial goods) [https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html=a07 "History and Traditions" Yokoshibahikari Town Homepage] | burial Structures = | map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Ogawadai
Kofun Group|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=20|lon_deg=140|lon_min=29|lon_sec=40|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|41|20|N|140|29|40|E}}}} }} '''Ogawadai Kofun Group''' (小川台古墳群) is a cluster of ancient Kofuns (kofun) located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Ogawadai Kofun Group is situated on a tongue-shaped plateau along the eastern midstream of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, in the central part of the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十九里浜}} region. It consists of 5 keyhole-shaped tumuli, 12 round tumuli, and 1 square tumulus. Among these, Kofun No. 1 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 28 meters), Kofun No. 2 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 21 meters), Kofun No. 3 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 24 meters), Kofun No. 4 (a square tumulus with a side length of approximately 14 meters), and Kofun No. 5 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 30 meters) were excavated in 1974. The largest mound in the group, Kofun No. 6, with a visible length of 42 meters, has yet to be investigated. == burial goods == Significant numbers of burial goods were uncovered during the excavations of Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 5. * '''Kofun No. 1:''' Three burial facilities yielded four iron swords, one iron spear, four iron arrowheads, one iron knife, ten perforated circular stone disks, 39 spindle-shaped beads, three iron arrowheads, one iron axe, and more than 151 spindle-shaped stone beads. * '''Kofun No. 5:''' burial goods discovered in a wooden coffin at the top of the rear circular portion include five long swords, one knife, one iron arrowhead, Hyōgo chains accompanying stirrups, one agate magatama (curved jewel), and 42 glass beads. The mound and surrounding moat contained numerous cylindrical haniwa, as well as human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, chicken-shaped, and waterfowl-shaped animal haniwa, in addition to house-shaped figurines and other symbolic haniwa. Analysis of their placement suggests that symbolic haniwa were concentrated along the northern middle section, extending from the front part of the keyhole-shaped mound to the rear circular portion, and were likely confined to specific sections of the mound. === Human-shaped Haniwa Fifteen human haniwa were identified from Kofun No. 5, including figures of armored warriors standing with both legs, male figures standing with both legs, seated female figures, and women carrying jars on their heads. Of these, 14 haniwa were classified by gender, with 9 males and 5 females, making them the oldest known human haniwa in Chiba Prefecture. While these haniwa are somewhat rough in craftsmanship, their details—particularly the horned helmets, armor, and hair styles—demonstrate considerable fidelity to actual objects. Notably, the standing warrior and male figures are unique in that their feet are directly attached to the mound without a pedestal, a feature rarely seen elsewhere. Similar examples include the wrestler haniwa from Harayama Kofun No. 1 in {{ill|Izumizaki Village|ja|泉崎村}}, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture, housed in the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Museum|ja|福島県立博物館}}. These rare burial goods were other_designation1 as town tangible cultural properties on April 1, 2018.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/419372 other_designation Database] == built == The burial goods from Kofun No. 1 suggest a 5th-century construction date, while the haniwa from Kofun No. 5 indicate a period from the late 5th century to the early 6th century. Kofun No. 4 yielded {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} suggesting a late 7th-century to early 8th-century date, possibly associated with secondary burials. While only five of the 18 mounds have been excavated, the group reflects construction over an extended period, spanning from the 5th to the 8th century. == References == * {{ill|Hajime Otsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, Saburo Kobayashi, and Masaya Kumano (Editors), ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 * {{ill|Reiji Harashima|ja|原島礼二}}, Ryoichi Kanaizuka (Editors), ''Eastern Japan and the Yamato Court,'' Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 1994. ISBN 4-642-02186-8, p. 141, "Human-shaped Haniwa of Ogawadai Kofun No. 5" == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-4.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibit on Ogawadai Kofun No. 5] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Ancient Japanese Kofuns in Chiba Prefecture]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | name = Ogawadai Kofun Group | image = | location = Ogawadai, Yokoshibahikari Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|20|N|140|29|40|E|region:JP_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumuli, round tumuli, square tumuli | area = 28 m, 30 m, 42 m | Era = 5th century – 7th century (early 8th century) | burial goods = Haniwa, others | alternate_name = Town-other_designation1 tangible cultural property (haniwa burial goods) [https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html=a07 "History and Traditions" Yokoshibahikari Town Homepage] | burial Structures = | map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Ogawadai
Kofun Group|coordinates=35|coordinates=41|coordinates=20|lon_deg=140|lon_min=29|lon_sec=40|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|41|20|N|140|29|40|E}}}} }} '''Ogawadai Kofun Group''' (小川台古墳群) is a cluster of ancient Kofuns (kofun) located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, Sanbu District, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Ogawadai Kofun Group is situated on a tongue-shaped plateau along the eastern midstream of the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, in the central part of the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十九里浜}} region. It consists of 5 keyhole-shaped tumuli, 12 round tumuli, and 1 square tumulus. Among these, Kofun No. 1 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 28 meters), Kofun No. 2 (a round tumulus with a diameter of 21 meters), Kofun No. 3 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 24 meters), Kofun No. 4 (a square tumulus with a side length of approximately 14 meters), and Kofun No. 5 (a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 30 meters) were excavated in 1974. The largest mound in the group, Kofun No. 6, with a visible length of 42 meters, has yet to be investigated. == burial goods == Significant numbers of burial goods were uncovered during the excavations of Kofun No. 1 and Kofun No. 5. * '''Kofun No. 1:''' Three burial facilities yielded four iron swords, one iron spear, four iron arrowheads, one iron knife, ten perforated circular stone disks, 39 spindle-shaped beads, three iron arrowheads, one iron axe, and more than 151 spindle-shaped stone beads. * '''Kofun No. 5:''' burial goods discovered in a wooden coffin at the top of the rear circular portion include five long swords, one knife, one iron arrowhead, Hyōgo chains accompanying stirrups, one agate magatama (curved jewel), and 42 glass beads. The mound and surrounding moat contained numerous cylindrical haniwa, as well as human-shaped, horse-shaped, deer-shaped, chicken-shaped, and waterfowl-shaped animal haniwa, in addition to house-shaped figurines and other symbolic haniwa. Analysis of their placement suggests that symbolic haniwa were concentrated along the northern middle section, extending from the front part of the keyhole-shaped mound to the rear circular portion, and were likely confined to specific sections of the mound. === Human-shaped Haniwa Fifteen human haniwa were identified from Kofun No. 5, including figures of armored warriors standing with both legs, male figures standing with both legs, seated female figures, and women carrying jars on their heads. Of these, 14 haniwa were classified by gender, with 9 males and 5 females, making them the oldest known human haniwa in Chiba Prefecture. While these haniwa are somewhat rough in craftsmanship, their details—particularly the horned helmets, armor, and hair styles—demonstrate considerable fidelity to actual objects. Notably, the standing warrior and male figures are unique in that their feet are directly attached to the mound without a pedestal, a feature rarely seen elsewhere. Similar examples include the wrestler haniwa from Harayama Kofun No. 1 in {{ill|Izumizaki Village|ja|泉崎村}}, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture, housed in the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Museum|ja|福島県立博物館}}. These rare burial goods were other_designation1 as town tangible cultural properties on April 1, 2018.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/419372 other_designation Database] == built == The burial goods from Kofun No. 1 suggest a 5th-century construction date, while the haniwa from Kofun No. 5 indicate a period from the late 5th century to the early 6th century. Kofun No. 4 yielded {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} suggesting a late 7th-century to early 8th-century date, possibly associated with secondary burials. While only five of the 18 mounds have been excavated, the group reflects construction over an extended period, spanning from the 5th to the 8th century. == References == * {{ill|Hajime Otsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, Saburo Kobayashi, and Masaya Kumano (Editors), ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. ISBN 4-490-10260-7 * ''Chiba Prefecture Place names (Japanese Historical Place names Series 12),'' Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1 * {{ill|Reiji Harashima|ja|原島礼二}}, Ryoichi Kanaizuka (Editors), ''Eastern Japan and the Yamato Court,'' Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 1994. ISBN 4-642-02186-8, p. 141, "Human-shaped Haniwa of Ogawadai Kofun No. 5" == See Also == * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|ja|芝山古墳・はにわ博物館}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/tenji/1-4.html Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum Exhibit on Ogawadai Kofun No. 5] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Ancient Japanese Kofuns in Chiba Prefecture]] Similar examples include the wrestler haniwa from Harayama Kofun No. 1 in {{ill|Izumizaki Village|ja|泉崎村}}, Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima Prefecture, housed in the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Museum|ja|福島県立博物館}}. These rare burial goods were other_designation1 as town tangible cultural properties on April 1, 2018.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/419372 other_designation Database] == built == The burial goods from Kofun No. 1 suggest a 5th-century construction date, while the haniwa from Kofun No. 5 indicate a period from the late 5th century to the early 6th century. Kofun No. 4 yielded {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} suggesting a late 7th-century to early 8th-century date, possibly associated with secondary burials. While only five of the 18 mounds have been excavated, the group reflects construction over an extended period, spanning from the 5th to the 8th century. == References == * {{ill|Hajime Otsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, Saburo Kobayashi, and Masaya Kumano (Editors), ''Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun,'' Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989.
